Home » State Senate District 29 — Democratic Primary

State Senate District 29 — Democratic Primary

Anne Carney is the only Democrat on the primary ballot. She advances directly to November.

What a State Senator does: One of 35 senators in Augusta. Votes on the two-year state budget and every state law. Your senator's vote determines: how much state money South Portland schools receive, your property tax relief, environmental regulations protecting Casco Bay, healthcare policy, and housing laws. District 29 covers Cape Elizabeth, South Portland, and part of Scarborough.

Committee Leadership

Carney chairs the Judiciary Committee and Joint Rules Committee. Also serves on Inland Fisheries and Wildlife. As Judiciary Chair, oversees bills on courts, civil rights, criminal justice, and data privacy.

Anne Carney

Incumbent Democrat
Since December 2020 · Cape Elizabeth · Campaign website · Legislature page

Platform

Gun Safety
Lead Senate sponsor of 2024 gun safety package after Lewiston shooting. Sponsored bump stock ban (LD 2086), 72-hour waiting period (LD 2238), expanded background checks, and strengthened violence prevention (LD 2237). Giffords Law Center upgraded Maine from D- to C+ — nation's "Most Improved."
Maine Senate Democrats
Environmental Protection
Sponsored first-in-the-nation law banning foam food containers. Bill requiring proper closure of oil terminal facilities. Voted for offshore wind (LD 1895), net energy billing reform (LD 1986), and protecting loons (LD 958).
Maine Conservation Voters scorecard
Civil Legal Aid
Sponsored LD 1022 to protect and increase access to civil legal services for low-income Mainers — $6.4M in FY25-26, $9.5M in FY26-27.
Maine Senate Democrats
Workers' Rights
Sponsored bill to protect pregnant workers. Served on Labor and Housing Committee.
LinkedIn
Democracy & Civil Rights
Voted for National Popular Vote (LD 1578), protecting voting rights (LD 1365), blocking censorship (LD 1518), and tribal court criminal jurisdiction (LD 2007). Co-Chair of Gun Safety Caucus.
MCV scorecard

Background

Practiced employment, civil rights, and municipal law at Norman, Hanson & DeTroy, LLC for 17 years. Before that, 8 years at Pine Tree Legal Assistance (pro bono). Chaired Cape Elizabeth Land Trust to national accreditation (2015). Maine AAP 2025 Legislator of the Year. Pine Tree Legal Assistance Award for Pro Bono Services (2016). Education: Haverford College, Harvard Divinity School, UMaine School of Law.

Endorsements

Maine Conservation Voters Action Fund (2020, 2024). Professional Firefighters of Maine. Maine AFL-CIO (2024). Maine Chapter of the American Academy of Pediatrics (2025 Legislator of the Year). Full list

Who You'd Face

Vincent Maietta (R) — South Portland business owner, uncontested in Republican primary. In 2013, indicted on perjury charges (see full profile).

Sources

Maine Legislature — Sen. Anne Carney · Maine Conservation Voters scorecard · Maine Senate Democrats — Gun safety · Carney endorsements

Press Herald — Maietta perjury indictment · Press Herald — DA drops Maietta case
